showFullCode.title: Show Complete File
showFullCode.description: Show complete file for code, with bootstrap and error handling included.

bitly_url.description: The unique bit.ly URL for this GIF

create_datetime.description: The date this GIF was added to the GIPHY database.

embded_url.description: A URL used for embedding this GIF

featured_tags.description: An array of featured tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

import_datetime.description: The creation or upload date from this GIF's source.

rating.description: The MPAA-style rating for this content. Examples include Y, G, PG, PG-13 and R

slug.description: The unique slug used in this GIF's URL

source.description: The page on which this GIF was found

source_post_url.description: The URL of the webpage on which this GIF was found.

source_tld.description: The top level domain of the source URL.

tags.description: An array of tags for this GIF (Note: Not available when using the Public Beta Key)

trending_datetime.description: The date on which this gif was marked trending, if applicable.

update_datetime.description: The date on which this GIF was last updated.

url.description: The unique URL for this GIF

username.description: The username this GIF is attached to, if applicable

frames.description: The number of frames in this GIF.

height.description: The height of this GIF in pixels.

mp4.description: The URL for this GIF in .MP4 format.

mp4_size.description: The size in bytes of the .MP4 file corresponding to this GIF.

size.description: The size of this GIF in bytes.

url.description: The publicly-accessible direct URL for this GIF.

webp.description: The URL for this GIF in .webp format.

webp_size.description: The size in bytes of the .webp file corresponding to this GIF.

width.description: The width of this GIF in pixels.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: The Meta Object contains basic information regarding the request, whether it was successful, and the response given by the API.  Check `responses` to see a description of types of response codes the API might give you under different cirumstances.

response_id.description: A unique ID paired with this response from the API.

Pagination.title: Pagination
Pagination.description: The Pagination Object contains information relating to the number of total results available as well as the number of results fetched and their relative positions.

User.title: User
User.description: The User Object contains information about the user associated with a GIF and URLs to assets such as that user's avatar image, profile, and more.

avatar_url.description: The URL for this user's avatar image.

banner_url.description: The URL for the banner image that appears atop this user's profile page.

display_name.description: The display name associated with this user (contains formatting the base username might not).

profile_url.description: The URL for this user's profile.

twitter.description: The Twitter username associated with this user, if applicable.

username.description: The username associated with this user.

Images.title: Images
Images.description: An object containing data for various available formats and sizes of this GIF.

Type.title: Type
Type.description: Type of the gif. By default, this is almost always gif

Nodes.Text: Giphy API
Official Giphy Documentation: [https://developers.giphy.com/docs/](https://developers.giphy.com/docs/)
Supported Java version is **8+**.

The generated code uses a few Maven dependencies e.g., Jackson, OkHttp,
and Apache HttpClient. The reference to these dependencies is already
added in the pom.xml file will be installed automatically. Therefore,
you will need internet access for a successful build.
* In order to open the client library in Eclipse click on `File -> Import`.
* In the import dialog, select `Existing Java Project` and click `Next`.
* Browse to locate the folder containing the source code. Select the detected location of the project and click `Finish`.
* Upon successful import, the project will be automatically built by Eclipse after automatically resolving the dependencies.

* After successfully building the project, the client library needs to be installed as a Maven package in your local cache. Right-click on the project, select `Show in Local Terminal -> Terminal` or use `Ctrl + Alt + T` to open Terminal.
* In the terminal dialog, run the following command to install client library.
mvn install -Dmaven.test.skip=true -Dmaven.javadoc.skip=true
The following section explains how to use the GiphyAPILib library in a new project.
For starting a new project, click the menu command `File > New > Project`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Add a new project in Eclipse

Nodes.Text: Next, choose `Maven > Maven Project` and click `Next`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Maven Project - Step 1

Nodes.Text: Here, make sure to use the current workspace by choosing `Use default Workspace location`, as shown in the picture below and click `Next`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Maven Project - Step 2

Nodes.Text: Following this, select the *quick start* project type to create a simple project with an existing class and a `main` method. To do this, choose `maven-archetype-quickstart` item from the list and click `Next`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Maven Project - Step 3

Nodes.Text: In the last step, provide a `Group Id` and `Artifact Id` as shown in the picture below and click `Finish`.

Nodes.AlternateText: Create a new Maven Project - Step 4

Nodes.Text: The created Maven project manages its dependencies using its `pom.xml` file. In order to add a dependency on the *GiphyAPILib* client library, double click on the ``` pom.xml ``` file in the ``` Package Explorer ```. Opening the ``` pom.xml ``` file will render a graphical view on the canvas. Here, switch to the `Dependencies` tab and click the `Add` button as shown in the picture below.

Nodes.AlternateText: Adding dependency to the client library - Step 1

Nodes.Text: Clicking the `Add` button will open a dialog where you need to specify GiphyAPILib in `Group Id`, giphy-apilib in `Artifact Id` and 1.0.0 in the `Version` fields. Once added click `OK`. Save the `pom.xml` file.

Nodes.AlternateText: Adding dependency to the client library - Step 2

Here, you can add code to initialize the client library and instantiate a *Controller* class. Sample code to initialize the client library and using controller methods is given in the subsequent sections.

The generated code and the server can be tested using automatically generated test cases. 
JUnit is used as the testing framework and test runner.
In Eclipse, for running the tests do the following:

1. Select the project GiphyAPILib from the package explorer.
2. Select `Run -> Run as -> JUnit Test` or use `Alt + Shift + X` followed by `T` to run the Tests.
